text:
A farmer named Tom lived in a quiet village surrounded by farms. One crisp autumn morning, he noticed something unusual happening across his fields. Every tree bore an eerie red blush, and even the pumpkins had a peculiar crimson hue. Concerned, Tom ventured into his expansive pumpkin patch to discover the source.

Standing amidst the orange orbs were a group of creatures he had never seen before - tiny creatures with large, round eyes and translucent wings. They scurried around happily, chattering among themselves in what Tom believed to be a form of language he couldn’t quite catch.

Tom approached cautiously, his heart pounding. “Who are you?” he whispered.

The creatures quivered, a symphony of wing flaps announcing their presence. Then one of them spoke in perfect English, “Hello, Tom. We are the Spectracutters. This autumn, our colors change to match the harvest cycle. We can’t resist pumpkins, so here we are.”
